published_prior_art.cite: Gunsilius2023DiSCo gives a weighted quantile-function counterfactual; Bai2009IFE gives mean interactive fixed effects, but neither states this monotone compatibility-set object

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_synth_control_factor_misspec_v1: scalar Chebyshev factor-misspecification bounds; panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: mean estimator equality frontier

published_prior_art.cite: Gunsilius2023DiSCo weighted quantile barycenter; ChenDoladoGonzalo2021QFM quantile-factor estimation; Masini2025DCA conditional-quantile estimation

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_synth_control_factor_misspec_v1: scalar partial-ID tube, not open-set distributional rank-factor escape

published_prior_art.cite: FermanPinto2021ImperfectFit imperfect mean-SC fit; Chen2020DistributionalSC residual distributional SC; neither gives exact-fit open-set distributional nonidentification
Gunsilius2023DiSCo: weighted donor quantile curve in the stable convex-loading corner

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Correct the Masini2025DCA bibliography metadata: verified Journal of Econometrics entry is Volume 249, Part A, May 2025, article 105675, not volume 246.

Add a brief comparison to Xu, Gao, Oka, and Whang 2025, Econometric Reviews, on quantile random-coefficient regression with interactive fixed effects; it is not subsuming, but it is a closer quantile-interactive-effects causal-panel comparator than Bai alone.

For Conjecture 2, state a minimal nondegenerate witness template with explicit J, T0, R and a positive-measure rank interval; this would make the open-set obstruction visibly generic rather than only topological.

recommended_next_step: Proceed to Stage 0 with Conjecture 1 as the flagship target and Conjecture 2 as the generic nonidentification companion.
